skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: A sample of maple has a density of 642 kilograms per cubic meter. The sample is in the shape of a cube, where each edge has a length of 0.85 meters. To the nearest whole number, what is the mass, in kilograms, of this sample?
rationale: The volume of the cube is (0.85 m)3 = 0.614125 cubic meters. Mass = density × volume = 642 × 0.614125 ≈ 394.27 kg, which rounds to 394. Choice (b) is correct.
